title: Same-window event extends/reopens the span with no idle check — the chatty-while-away inflation the consume docstring claims to prevent
detail: Verified. `on_focus` (269-296) never consults idle: same-window (282-288) calls `set_span_end(ev.timestamp)` + `open.end = ev.timestamp` unconditionally; the switch/reopen path (291-294) calls `open_span` for any Track event. The idle gate is ONLY in `on_tick` (316), which `consume` runs only when `now >= next_tick` (370), ~every TICK_SECS=20s. Between ticks on_focus extends freely. D32 (decisions.md:151) and the consume docstring (343-345) both claim idle is checked 'on every loop wake' to stop a chatty live-title tab ballooning the span — but the check is per-tick, not per-wake, and the away
fix: Gate on_focus on idle like on_tick: read current_idle_secs() on event arrival in consume and pass into on_focus; if idle >= gate_secs_for(focus.app), close any open span at its existing end, set current=None, keep last_focus, do NOT extend or open. Keep on_tick the single judge but have on_focus honor the same deadline. Tighten the docstring. Add a test: a stream of same-app distinct-title events

title: FocusEvent.bundle_id is a dead carrier field — set by macOS source, never read
detail: Confirmed write-only. FocusEvent.bundle_id (35) is set at macos/mod.rs:217 but a grep for reads of the FocusEvent field returns nothing — db.rs, enrich.rs, and the consumer state machine never read ev.bundle_id. The macOS impl uses its own self.bundle_id (206, 214) for browser/terminal enrichment before building the event, so carrying it across the channel earns nothing — leftover plumbing. D39 confirms FocusEvent is capture-internal (no IPC/bindings impact), so removal is IPC-safe. Not autofixable: removal touches macos/mod.rs:217 and the test helper focus() at mod.rs:399-407 — a multi-file l
fix: Delete the bundle_id field from FocusEvent (35) and the `bundle_id: Some(self.bundle_id.clone())` line in macos/mod.rs emit_with_title (217). Verify cargo build/clippy/test stay green.

title: FocusEvent.pid is a dead carrier field — set by macOS source, never read by the consumer
detail: Confirmed write-only, same shape as bundle_id. FocusEvent.pid (36) is set by polling.rs:58 and macos/mod.rs:218, and the test helper at mod.rs:403 sets pid:1. Nothing downstream reads ev.pid: resolve_focus, on_focus, on_tick, open_span, db.rs, enrich.rs never reference it. The macOS source uses its own self.pid (214,249,251) before building the event — the field is never consumed. Not autofixable: removal touches polling.rs:58, macos/mod.rs:218, and the test helper mod.rs:403.
fix: Delete the pid field from FocusEvent (36) and its three assignments: polling.rs:58 (`pid: window.process_id as i32`), macos/mod.rs:218 (`pid: self.pid`), test helper mod.rs:403 (`pid: 1`). Verify gates stay green.

title: emit_with_title spawns osascript/pgrep synchronously on the main run loop — an unresponsive scripted app freezes the UI
detail: Verified. emit_with_title (mod.rs:202-225) calls browser::inspect (line 206) and terminal::front_cwd (line 214) synchronously. browser::inspect → run_osa (browser.rs:70-81) does Command::new(OSASCRIPT)...output() with NO timeout; terminal::front_cwd → iterm2_cwd (terminal.rs:28-32) and child_pids (terminal.rs:56-60) likewise spawn /usr/bin/osascript and /usr/bin/pgrep with .output() and no timeout. emit_with_title is reached from (a) observer_callback (line 322, ctx.emit → emit_with_title), documented to run on the main run loop (module header lines 6-9, callback doc line 297), and (b) switch_
fix: Move the osascript/pgrep enrichment off the main thread: emit a FocusEvent carrying only the AX title (cheap, pure AX) and resolve url/cwd on the consume() worker thread, OR bound each subprocess with a timeout (e.g. spawn + wait_timeout) so a stuck browser/terminal cannot pin the run loop. Do not just add a timeout silently — keep the title path synchronous so titles are never lost.

title: Redundant manual new() alongside #[derive(Default)] on a unit marker
detail: Verified. MacosCapture is a zero-field unit struct with both #[derive(Default)] (line 52) and a hand-written impl with pub fn new() -> Self { Self } (lines 55-59). The only call site is macos::MacosCapture::new() in capture/mod.rs:91. Two constructors for a unit marker is pure ceremony.
fix: Drop the manual impl MacosCapture { pub fn new() ... } block (lines 55-59) and change the sole call site (capture/mod.rs:91) to MacosCapture::default(); or drop #[derive(Default)] and keep new(). Keep exactly one. Note clippy under -D warnings may flag new_without_default if you keep new() without Default, so removing new() and using default() is the cleaner direction.

title: focused_window_title and copy_focused_window both re-copy AXFocusedWindow
detail: Verified. focused_window_title (mod.rs:353-368) and copy_focused_window (371-376) both call copy_attr(app_el, ATTR_FOCUSED_WINDOW) then do an unsafe CFRetained::as_ptr(...).cast::<AXUIElement>() pointer dance — focused_window_title borrows the ref (line 356-358), copy_focused_window retains it (line 373-375). Two near-identical unsafe casts of the AXFocusedWindow value; getting one subtly wrong while the other stays correct is exactly the FFI footgun worth consolidating.
fix: Have focused_window_title call copy_focused_window(app_el) to obtain the owned window element, then read ATTR_TITLE from it — one copy-and-cast path. Verify the existing on-device behavior is preserved (retain is sound; the borrow variant is the riskier one being eliminated). Run cargo test + clippy after.

title: reprocess_logs (SQL LIKE) and find_category (Rust substring) categorize the same rows differently
detail: Confirmed against the code and the documented contract. find_category (db.rs:408-411) matches with `match_target.to_lowercase().contains(&rule.pattern.to_lowercase())` — a literal substring test. reprocess_logs (db.rs:429-439) instead builds `format!("%{}%", rule.pattern)` and runs `lower(process_name|window_title) LIKE lower(?2)` with NO `ESCAPE` clause. In SQLite LIKE, `_` matches any single character and `%` matches any run, so a pattern containing `_` or `%` (e.g. the literal project/window name `usage_os`) matches MORE rows under reprocess than under live capture. Because reprocess_logs i
fix: Make both paths use identical substring semantics. Preferred: drop LIKE and reprocess in Rust — read candidate rows and reuse find_category's `.contains()` so there is ONE matcher. If keeping SQL LIKE, escape the meta-chars and add an ESCAPE clause: `let p = rule.pattern.replace('\\',"\\\\").replace('%',"\\%").replace('_',"\\_"); let pattern = format!("%{p}%");` and append `ESCAPE '\\'` to both LI

title: get_setting / find_project_by_alias / get_project repeat the same query-one-row dance
detail: Confirmed. get_setting (db.rs:546-554), find_project_by_alias (db.rs:576-590), and get_project (db.rs:646-662) each hand-roll `prepare` + `query` + `if let Some(row) = rows.next()? { Ok(Some(..)) } else { Ok(None) }`. The exact `if let Some(row) = rows.next()?` shape appears at db.rs:549, 585, 651 (grep-confirmed, 3 instances). rusqlite's `query_row(...).optional()` does this in one expression and is already used in this same file two functions away in resolve_or_create_project (db.rs:619-625) — so the verbose form is inconsistent with the established idiom. OptionalExtension is already import
fix: Rewrite each with query_row(...).optional(): get_setting → `conn.query_row(SQL, [key], |row| row.get(0)).optional()`; find_project_by_alias → `conn.query_row(SQL, (alias_kind, alias_value), |row| row.get(0)).optional()`; get_project → `conn.query_row(SQL, [id], |row| Ok(Project{ ... })).optional()`. Run cargo test (export/settings/project tests cover these) + clippy/fmt before merge.

title: Drift check silently ignores a stored migration absent from MIGRATIONS (binary downgrade)
detail: Confirmed. verify_applied_checksums (line 97) does `if let Some(migration) = MIGRATIONS.iter().find(|m| m.version == version)` — a schema_migrations row whose version is not in the binary's MIGRATIONS array is silently skipped. For a forward-only design (D35), the dangerous direction is the on-disk DB being AHEAD of the running binary (a downgrade/rollback or a user opening an old build), where the schema may contain columns/tables the binary doesn't expect. The checksum guard catches edits to known migrations but not 'DB is from the future'. This is a genuine gap, though low-likelihood for a
fix: Add a guard before running: if current_version(conn) > MIGRATIONS.last().version, return a clear 'database was created by a newer version of UsageOS' error. Or, if intentionally tolerated, add a one-line comment at line 97 stating rows beyond the binary's chain are accepted.

title: `(SELECT COUNT(*) FROM activity_logs) = 0` fresh-install guard repeated on every statement
detail: Confirmed. The fresh-install gate `(SELECT COUNT(*) FROM activity_logs) = 0` is repeated on every mutating statement: lines 12, 13, 14, 15 (the four UPDATE name changes), 21 (the Personal INSERT), 27 (the DELETE), and 42 (the rules INSERT-SELECT). The migration's own header (lines 6-7) promises 'every statement is gated ... so this migration ONLY ever changes a fresh database' — and that invariant rests entirely on every statement carrying the guard identically. A future statement added without the guard would silently rewrite an existing user's categories/rules — the exact harm the header rul
fix: Add a test asserting a DB seeded with an activity_logs row is untouched by 0006 (names, Personal absence, rules all unchanged) to lock the invariant. Optionally restructure so the empty-DB gate is expressed once (e.g. a temp guard table populated only when empty, joined into each statement).

title: 127.0.0.1 with a port is not treated as a local dev server (asymmetry with localhost)
detail: Confirmed. Line 176 reads `host == "localhost" || host == "127.0.0.1" || host.starts_with("localhost:")`. parse_url (line 318-319) keeps the port on host: for `http://127.0.0.1:3002/`, rest.split_once('/') yields host=`127.0.0.1:3002`, which equals neither `127.0.0.1` (exact) nor matches `localhost:` prefix, so it falls through line 184/185 to Classified::NoSignal. By contrast `http://localhost:3002/` is caught by the starts_with arm → Ambiguous. So the loopback-IP-with-port dev server (the `vite --host`/Docker case) is mislabeled no-signal, losing the D30 ambiguous-correlation signal that the
fix: Strip the port before comparing, mirroring parse_site: `let bare = host.split(':').next().unwrap_or(host); if bare == "localhost" || bare == "127.0.0.1" {` and drop the now-redundant starts_with arm. Add `http://127.0.0.1:3002/` to the ambiguous test array. Not autofixable (logic change touching classification behavior + needs a test).

title: github.com matched with ends_with — subdomain/lookalike host slips through
detail: Confirmed. Line 159 is `if host.ends_with("github.com")`. A lookalike host such as `notgithub.com` or `evilgithub.com` satisfies ends_with and enters the owner/repo parser (lines 160-171), minting a bogus canonical `owner/repo` project from a non-GitHub host. owner/repo extraction is only meaningful for the real github.com (and www.github.com). The other ends_with uses (DEV_DASHBOARDS line 179, GENERAL_HOSTS line 182) are intentional subdomain matching where any subdomain is fine, but here the literal path-shape assumption makes ends_with wrong. Re-labeling as correctness (a wrong project gets
fix: Match exact host: `if host == "github.com" || host == "www.github.com" {`. Note host may carry a port from parse_url, but GitHub URLs in practice have none; if defensiveness is wanted, strip port first. Not autofixable (logic change).

title: cache_key collisions can serve one app's icon to a different app
detail: Verified real. Dedup in collect_dir (apps.rs:112) keys on stem.to_lowercase(), so "Foo Bar" and "Foo-Bar" are distinct keys and BOTH survive dedup. But the cache filename uses cache_key(stem) (apps.rs:133, 189-193), which maps every non-ASCII-alphanumeric char to '_': cache_key("Foo Bar") == cache_key("Foo-Bar") == "Foo_Bar". Non-ASCII collapses too ("Café"/"Cafe"). When app B is processed after app A wrote the colliding PNG, is_cached_fresh (apps.rs:196) compares B's icns mtime against A's PNG mtime; if A's PNG is newer, sips is skipped and B reads A's PNG (apps.rs:147) — B is shown A's icon.
fix: Make the filename injective per stem: append a short stable hash of the original stem, e.g. format!("{}-{:08x}.png", cache_key(stem), hash_of(stem)), or hex-encode the non-alphanumeric bytes instead of collapsing each to '_'. Either stays filesystem-safe while keeping distinct names distinct. Must not panic / add a dep beyond std (a tiny inline FNV-1a fold suffices, matching the project's no-new-d

title: useDayData has no out-of-order / stale-response guard — fast day navigation can render the wrong day
detail: Confirmed. fetchDay (L22-31) awaits getDay then unconditionally setData (L25); the effect (L33-39) re-runs on [fetchDay, live] (fetchDay is keyed on [startUnix,endUnix]) and a 30s setInterval (L37) also fires it. The effect cleanup only clears the interval (L38), nothing cancels/ignores the in-flight promise. So if the range changes (or a poll for day A resolves) after navigating to day B, A's response lands last and overwrites B. The pattern guard already exists elsewhere in the subsystem: useInstalledApps uses an `alive` flag (useInstalledApps.ts L34-40) and DayView's watcher effect uses `ca
fix: Capture a per-run flag in the effect: `let alive = true;` and have fetchDay only setData/setError when alive; set alive=false in cleanup (alongside clearInterval). Best folded into a shared useRangeData (see fe-views-s1) so all three hooks are fixed once. Cosmetic stale data only, no panic/SQL/IPC impact.

title: useTimelineData shares the same stale-response race as useDayData
detail: Confirmed, identical shape. fetchTimeline (L25-34) awaits getTimeline and unconditionally setData (L28); effect (L36-42) re-runs on range change + 30s poll (L40); cleanup only clearInterval (L41). No cancelled/alive guard, so a slow timeline response for a prior day can land after navigation and render under the new header.
fix: Mirror the useDayData fix: per-run alive flag gating setData/setError, set false in cleanup. Or extract the shared useRangeData hook (fe-views-s1) to fix all three.

title: useWeekData shares the same stale-response race
detail: Confirmed. fetchWeek (L23-33) does setData(await getWeek(starts, weekEnd)) (L27) with no guard; effect (L35-41) re-runs on [fetchWeek, live] + 30s poll (L39); cleanup only clearInterval (L40). A slow response for the previous week can land after the user moves week and desync the 7 mini-dials/aggregates from the WeekNav title.
fix: Add the same per-run alive guard before setData/setError, set false in cleanup. Or consolidate into useRangeData (fe-views-s1).

title: Pointless join→split round-trip of dayStarts to make a stable dep key
detail: Confirmed. useWeekData.ts L21 `const key = dayStarts.join(',')` then L26 inside fetchWeek `const starts = key.split(',').map(Number)`. The stringify/parse exists only to give useCallback a primitive dep (L33 deps [key, weekEnd]). WeekView already memoizes dayStarts (WeekView.tsx L24 useMemo), so the array identity is stable per week. The number→string→number round-trip is brittle and obscure for no benefit.
fix: Keep `key` in the dep list for a stable primitive but call getWeek with the array directly: `setData(await getWeek(dayStarts, weekEnd))` and drop the split. Or, since WeekView memoizes the array, depend on `[dayStarts, weekEnd]` and delete `key` entirely. Verify tsc/vitest still pass (pure refactor).

title: useDayData / useTimelineData are byte-for-byte identical except the fetch fn and error string
detail: Confirmed. useDayData.ts and useTimelineData.ts are structurally identical: same DayDataState/TimelineDataState shape (data/loading/error/refresh), same (start,end,live) signature, same fetchX keyed on [startUnix,endUnix], same effect (setLoading(true)+void fetch+30s interval, cleanup clearInterval), same return object. Only the imported getDay/getTimeline and the fallback string ('Couldn't load this day.' vs 'Couldn't load this timeline.') differ. useWeekData is the same modulo its array key. Three near-identical files means each fix (e.g. the race in r0/r1/r2) must be made three times.
fix: Extract a generic `useRangeData<T>(fetcher, deps, live, fallbackMsg)` and make the three hooks one-line wrappers; fold the stale-response guard (r0-r2) into it so all three are fixed at once. Pure-logic refactor — keep vitest coverage green.

title: Identical getWatcherStatus capture-health effect copy-pasted across all three views
detail: Confirmed. The same effect — getWatcherStatus().then(s => !cancelled && setCaptureHealthy(s.healthy)).catch(()=>undefined) with a cancelled flag — plus the same useState(true) and the same <DegradedBanner title="Tracking hit a snag" description="UsageOS ran into repeated errors while recording. Your existing data is safe." actionLabel="Retry" onAction={refresh}/> block appears verbatim in DayView (effect L41-49, banner L70-79), WeekView (effect L32-40, banner L57-66), and TimelineView (effect L27-35, banner L41-50). Three copies of identical logic + user-facing copy.
fix: Extract a `useCaptureHealth(rangeKey)` hook returning `healthy` and a `<CaptureDegradedBanner onRetry={refresh}/>` carrying the shared copy; each view drops ~10 lines of effect + ~10 of JSX. Doing this is also the natural place to fix fe-views-r4 (poll health on the 30s interval). Pure-frontend; keep tsc/vitest green.

title: ErrorState component is the exact same code in DayView, WeekView, and TimelineView
detail:
Confirmed verbatim. ErrorState({message,onRetry}) — dashed-border wrapper, muted message

, 'Try again' button with identical Tailwind classes — is defined three times: DayView.tsx L261-274, WeekView.tsx L205-218, TimelineView.tsx L211-224. Identical down to class strings and copy. A token/microcopy tweak must be made in three places.
fix: Promote to src/components/ui/ErrorState.tsx, import in all three views, delete the local copies. Straightforward; keep tsc/vitest green.

title: Ledger onMouseLeave calls onHover(isolated) — passes the live value back, so hover-dim sticks when nothing is pinned
detail: Confirmed bug. In Ledger (DayView.tsx L195-231), onHover IS setIsolated (passed L137) and there is a single `isolated` state used for BOTH click-pin (Chip toggleIsolate L64/L125, LedgerRow onClick L225) and hover. onMouseEnter (L223) calls onHover(row.slug) → setIsolated(row.slug), which re-renders Ledger; the new render's onMouseLeave closure (L224) now captures isolated === row.slug, so leaving calls onHover(row.slug) — a no-op. Result: with nothing pinned, hovering a row dims the others and leaving does NOT clear it (the dim sticks). The intent (restore prior state on leave) fails because t
fix: Separate pinned from transient hover. Simplest: track a `pinned` state (set by click/Chip) and a `hovered` state (set by enter, cleared to null on leave); compute the active isolation as `hovered ?? pinned`. Then onMouseLeave={() => setHovered(null)} correctly clears transient hover while preserving a pin. Logic change — verify the Chip active-state (L124 `active={isolated === c.slug}`) and Dial i

title: Leftover 'ctx' naming from the Context→Category rename (D46)
detail: Confirmed and squarely against a documented intent. SettingsView.tsx uses `ctx` as the map variable (L157), the CategoryRow prop `ctx: Category` (L280/L284), `ctx.id/ctx.slug/ctx.color/ctx.name` (L288-317), and `ctxRules` (L289, L300, L303). D46 explicitly renamed Context→Category EVERYWHERE — 'UI copy, IPC commands/types, Rust internals, frontend identifiers, and component/file names' — to heal the naming split; `ctx` against a `Category` type is exactly the half-renamed leftover the rename was meant to eliminate. Not a deliberate-keep (the SQL `category_id` legacy name is the only intentiona
fix: Rename `ctx`→`category` (or `cat`) and `ctxRules`→`categoryRules` in the map callback (L157-163) and CategoryRow (L279-317): e.g. `categories.map((category) => <CategoryRow key={category.id} category={category} rules={rules} onEdit={() => setEditing({category})}/>)`. Pure rename; keep tsc green. Not auto-fixable (identifier rename, not a mechanical fmt/dead-import fix).

title: Hardcoded rgba() bar-muted color bypasses the token system
detail: Confirmed at line 5: `const BAR_MUTED = "rgba(255,255,255,0.65)"`, applied at line 49 `style={{ color: BAR_MUTED }}` on the TrackingStatus text. Verified by grep it is the ONLY rgba(...) literal in the entire src tree. tokens.css defines `--bar-bg`/`--bar-fg` per theme (paper/warm/black blocks) but no `--bar-muted`. So this component invents a muted-on-dark color inline, outside the token vocabulary. It reads acceptably today (the bar is dark in all three themes) but will drift if the bar palette ever changes per-theme. Note D42 documents that Tailwind opacity modifiers do NOT work on the hex
fix: Add a `--bar-muted` token to each [data-theme] block in src/styles/tokens.css (a muted-on-dark value per theme) and replace `style={{ color: BAR_MUTED }}` with `style={{ color: 'var(--bar-muted)' }}`, deleting the literal. Do NOT use a Tailwind /opacity modifier on a bar-fg token — D42 shows those don't resolve on the hex var() tokens.

title: Expanded header labels segment count as "switches", contradicting countSwitches
detail: Confirmed. Line 84: `{run.segments.length} app {run.segments.length === 1 ? "switch" : "switches"}` labels the raw segment count as "switches". Lines 53-54 in the collapsed view show `{countSwitches(run.segments)} switches`, and countSwitches (lines 140-148) counts adjacent app/project transitions (for N segments with all-distinct apps that is N-1). So the same run shows different numbers for the word "switch" in the two surfaces (e.g. 3 segments → collapsed "2 switches", expanded "3 app switches"), and the expanded label is semantically off (N segments are N app visits, not N switches). This
fix: Relabel the expanded header to what it counts, e.g. `{run.segments.length} app {run.segments.length === 1 ? "visit" : "visits"}` (or "session(s)"), OR show `countSwitches(run.segments)` to match the collapsed line. Pick one definition of "switch" and use it in both places.

title: Modal does not trap focus or restore focus on close
detail: Confirmed. The open effect (lines 21-30) adds Esc-to-close and calls `dialogRef.current?.focus()` (line 28), but never (a) traps Tab inside the dialog nor (b) captures/restores the previously-focused element on close. The container sets `role="dialog" aria-modal="true"` (lines 43-45), which tells assistive tech focus is contained when it is not — Tab can leave the dialog into the page behind the dimmed overlay, and on close focus falls to <body> instead of the trigger. The Modal is the shared shell for CategoryEditorModal/ExclusionModal/DeleteAllModal, so this affects all settings dialogs. Thi
fix: In the open effect, capture `const prev = document.activeElement as HTMLElement | null` before `dialogRef.current?.focus()`, and in the cleanup call `prev?.focus()`. Add a keydown handler intercepting Tab/Shift+Tab to cycle among the dialog's focusable descendants (focus-trap), or adopt a small headless dialog primitive. At minimum restore focus on close.

title: Custom listbox has no keyboard navigation
detail: Confirmed. The trigger advertises `aria-haspopup="listbox"`/`aria-expanded` (lines 52-53) and the popover is `role="listbox"` (line 64) with `role="option"` children (line 71). The only keyboard handler is Esc-closes (lines 37-39). There is no ArrowUp/ArrowDown to move an active option, no Enter/Space to commit, no Home/End, and no `aria-activedescendant`; options are operable only by mouse click (line 74 onClick). This diverges from RadioGroup (role=radio buttons, focusable) in the same UI folder. It is a genuine keyboard/SR gap for a control presenting as a combobox. Not a documented deliber
fix: Handle ArrowDown/ArrowUp to move an active index, Enter/Space to commit, Home/End to jump, and reflect the active option via `aria-activedescendant` pointing at the focused option's id. Move keyboard focus into the menu on open or keep it on the trigger and drive selection with aria-activedescendant. Keep Esc-closes.

title: Expand header switch count contradicts the collapsed summary count
detail: Confirmed and is the same defect as fe-components-r1 (kept separate per instruction to adjudicate each id). Collapsed (line 53) shows `countSwitches(run.segments)` (transitions, gated >1); expanded header (lines 83-84) shows `run.segments.length` labeled "switch/switches". segments.length is the segment count, not the switch count, so the two figures disagree for the same run. One of them mislabels its quantity.
fix: Pick one definition. If the header means app sessions, reword to `{run.segments.length} {run.segments.length === 1 ? "app session" : "app sessions"}`. If it means switches, use countSwitches(run.segments) like the collapsed row. Don't label a segment count as a switch count.

title: Day vs Week use inconsistent day-end semantics; Week can double-count a midnight event
detail: Verified end-to-end. dayBounds (dates.ts:7) returns end = local 23:59:59. WeekView.tsx:24-25 passes the 7 dayBounds().start values and weekEnd=dayBounds(days[6]).end into getWeek. Rust get_week (lib.rs:133) sets each interior day's end to `day_starts.get(i+1)` = the NEXT day's local midnight. db::get_activity_logs (db.rs:264) filters `start_time >= ?1 AND start_time <= ?2` — INCLUSIVE on both ends. So an event whose start_time is exactly local midnight T is included in day i's slice ([start_i, T], T<=T) AND day i+1's slice ([T, end], T>=T). build_week_view (rollup.rs:261) sums per-day active_s
fix: Adopt one half-open convention across both views. Cleanest: make dayBounds return end = next-local-midnight (exclusive) and change the Rust filter to `start_time >= ?1 AND start_time < ?2` (get_week already passes next-midnight as interior end, so it becomes correct; get_day's caller passes the same exclusive end). Add a test asserting sum over Week day-slices == 7 standalone Day totals for the sa

title: Misleading "exclusive end" doc for getWeek contradicts dayBounds
detail: Verified. tauri.ts:81 JSDoc says "`weekEnd` is the exclusive end of the last day." The only caller (WeekView.tsx:25) passes `dayBounds(days[6]).end` = 23:59:59, and the Rust query (db.rs:264) is inclusive (`start_time <= ?2`). So weekEnd is an inclusive 23:59:59 bound, not exclusive — the comment describes a contract the code doesn't implement. A future reader passing a true exclusive midnight would silently shift behavior. The SAME wrong 'exclusive end' wording also appears in the Rust doc at lib.rs:113, so this is a two-site doc lie, not one line. (If fe-lib-r0/s1 are fixed by moving to a re
fix: Correct both docs to match current reality: "`weekEnd` is the inclusive upper bound (Unix secs) of the last day (23:59:59), matching `dayBounds().end`." If the team instead fixes the semantics (fe-lib-r0), make the code exclusive and leave the doc as-is. Either way the doc must not lie.

title: Prebuilt sidecar binary is untracked AND not gitignored — would be committed by `git add .`
detail: Confirmed. `git status --short` shows `?? src-tauri/binaries/` (untracked); `git check-ignore src-tauri/binaries/usageos-ai-aarch64-apple-darwin` exits 1 (NOT ignored). The root `.gitignore` covers `node_modules`, `dist`, `.build/` (SPM caches) but has no `src-tauri/binaries/` rule; `src-tauri/.gitignore` only ignores `/target/` and `/gen/schemas`. `file` confirms it is a 111KB Mach-O arm64 executable. `grep externalBin src-tauri/tauri.conf.json` returns nothing (exit 1) — Tauri does not consume it, consistent with the dormant chunk-D seam (D49). So an aarch64-only compiled blob is one `git ad
fix:
Append to root `.gitignore`:

# Prebuilt AI sidecar (built from spikes/foundation-models; wired via externalBin in chunk D)
src-tauri/binaries/

When the sidecar is wired in chunk D, commit it deliberately (or build in CI) and add the externalBin entry then. Adding an ignore rule is safe and touches no merge gate.

title: Content-Security-Policy is disabled (`csp: null`) in a privacy-first app
detail: Confirmed at tauri.conf.json:25 — `app.security.csp` is literally `null`, so Tauri injects no CSP. The frontend is fully local (frontendDist ../dist, local fonts @fontsource/anton + jost, custom SVG dial, no chart CDN), so today there is no outbound call — this is defense-in-depth, not an active leak (severity warning is right). A locked CSP is the runtime enforcement of hard rule 1 at the webview boundary, where an accidental future `fetch()` or a compromised JS dependency could egress with nothing stopping it. This is the higher-value of the two CSP findings; merged with config-s0. CAUTION o
fix: Set an explicit restrictive CSP, e.g. `"csp": "default-src 'self'; connect-src 'self' ipc: http://ipc.localhost; img-src 'self' data:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; font-src 'self' data:; script-src 'self'"`. Verify against a running build that IPC and the Bauhaus fonts still load before merging; tune connect-src to the actual Tauri v2 IPC origin.

title: opener:default grants allow-open-url (open arbitrary http/https) — over-broad, unused
detail: Confirmed. capabilities/default.json:9 lists `"opener:default"`, and line 10 separately lists `"opener:allow-reveal-item-in-dir"`. I read the plugin's own permissions/default.toml from the cargo cache: `[default]` expands to `allow-open-url`, `allow-reveal-item-in-dir`, `allow-default-urls`. So `opener:default` already includes reveal-item, making line 10 redundant, AND it grants `allow-open-url` (hand arbitrary mailto/tel/http/https URLs to the OS default app). The frontend only ever calls `revealItemInDir` (SettingsView.tsx:1 import, used at lines 108 and 118); grep finds no `openUrl`/`open(
fix: In src-tauri/capabilities/default.json delete line 9 `"opener:default"` and keep line 10 `"opener:allow-reveal-item-in-dir"`. Then run the app once to confirm reveal-in-Finder still works (the only opener call). Not a blind autofix — it's a capability-surface change.

title: Coalescing ignores cwd/project signal changes (same_window omits project fields)
detail: Confirmed and sharpened. same_window (line 147) compares app/title/url/is_private only. Focus also carries site, project_id, project_abstain_reason. site is derived purely from url (parse_site), so a site change cannot occur without a url change — that part of Codex's claim is moot. But project_id is inferred from cwd/url/title (resolve_focus -> infer_project with ProjectSignals{cwd,url,title}), and cwd is NOT in the Focus identity nor in same_window. Real gap for terminals: cd to a different git repo in the SAME terminal window while the AX title stays identical yields a different project_id,
fix: Include project_id (and project_abstain_reason) in the same_window comparison so a project change opens a new span. site is url-derived so redundant but harmless to add. Pair with fixing the producer dedupe so a cwd-only change actually re-emits a FocusEvent (otherwise on_focus never sees it).

title: Producer title-only dedupe suppresses URL / cwd / private-state changes
detail: Confirmed, strongest of the set. emit() (lines 187-197) reads only the AX title, compares to last_title, and returns early on an unchanged title BEFORE emit_with_title calls browser::inspect or terminal::front_cwd. So with an unchanged window title: (a) navigating a SPA/same-title page to a new URL, (b) cd-ing to a different repo in a terminal (new cwd -> new project), and (c) a Chromium window flipping normal<->incognito while the title text is unchanged all fail to emit a FocusEvent. The consumer keeps extending the prior span with stale url/site/project/is_private. Real signal loss; it COMP
fix: Build the full capture identity (title + url + is_private + cwd) first, then dedupe on the whole tuple — move browser::inspect and terminal::front_cwd ahead of the dedupe check and compare a struct, not just the title. Alternative: drop producer-side dedupe and let the consumer's same_window coalescing be the single source of truth (but then also fix same_window to include project_id/url signals).

title: Range reads miss/overcount spans crossing day boundaries
detail: Real on main. get_activity_logs filters `start_time >= ?1 AND start_time <= ?2` (both inclusive) and rollup::duration (rollup.rs:157) counts the FULL span `(end_time - start_time)`, never clipped to the window. Spans are NOT split at midnight (capture/mod.rs: open_span→set_span_end runs focus-change to focus-change). So a 23:50→00:10 span: counted wholly in the 23:50 day (overcounts +10m) and missing from the next day. A span starting exactly at the next midnight (start_time == prev day's `end`) satisfies both `<= end` (day i) and `>= start` (day i+1) → double-counted. This is per-day rollup s
fix: Use overlap+clip semantics for rollup reads: `WHERE end_time > ?1 AND start_time < ?2`, and clip each returned span to [start,end] before summing (clamp start_time=max(start_time,window_start), end_time=min(end_time,window_end)) so a crossing span is split correctly across days. Make boundary inclusive on one side only (half-open [start,end)).

title: Empty/whitespace rule pattern matches every event
detail: Real on main. create_rule inserts pattern with no trim/non-empty check. find_category (db.rs:408) does `match_target.to_lowercase().contains(&rule.pattern.to_lowercase())` — empty pattern → contains("") → always true; reprocess_logs (db.rs:432/437) does `LIKE lower('%%')` → always true. So an empty-pattern rule sorts ALL history into one category. D45 added conflict-detection as a frontend UI affordance (processOwner) but explicitly did NOT add empty-pattern rejection at the repository boundary, so this backend gap is real and not a covered deliberate choice. User-triggered misconfiguration, f
fix: In create_rule, reject empty/whitespace patterns: `if pattern.trim().is_empty() { return Err(...) }` (add a typed repo error variant). Add a focused test. Optionally a CHECK(length(trim(pattern))>0) in the next migration.

title: Empty exclusion pattern drops or privatizes every capture
detail: Real on main and the most severe of this set. create_exclusion has no trim/non-empty guard. match_exclusion (db.rs:801) does `!target.contains(&ex.pattern.to_lowercase())` — empty pattern → contains("") → matches everything. An empty `exclude` rule makes EVERY captured event get dropped (never written — Resolved::Excluded path), so the day silently goes blank with no recovery of the lost spans; an empty `private` rule blanks ALL titles/urls at write time. This is data-loss + a privacy/correctness footgun, user-triggerable via the exclusion modal. Not covered by D8 (D8 is about the exclusion me
fix: In create_exclusion, reject empty/whitespace patterns before insert (typed error). Add a CHECK(length(trim(pattern))>0) constraint in the next migration for defense in depth, and a test asserting an empty pattern is rejected (not stored).

title: Enum-like columns documented but not enforced
detail: Real on main. rules.match_field, exclusions.match_type, exclusions.mode, sites.kind, activity_logs.project_abstain_reason are comment-only enums with no CHECK constraints; the repository accepts arbitrary strings. Invalid values are silently mis-handled: find_category treats any match_field != 'process' as a title rule (db.rs:402 else-branch), match_exclusion `continue`s on an unknown match_type/mode (db.rs:798/807) so the rule silently no-ops. This is the root shape behind findings 2/3 (no boundary validation). Note: recaps.generated_by DOES use a CHECK per standards/rust.md:160, so the patte
fix: Add repository-side validation now (reject unknown match_field/match_type/mode/kind with a typed error) and add CHECK(col IN (...)) constraints in the next forward-only migration where the existing data is known-clean. Mirror the generated_by CHECK pattern.

title: History reprocessing is not atomic
detail: Real on main. reprocess_logs first runs `UPDATE activity_logs SET category_id = NULL` (db.rs:420) then applies each rule in a separate execute with no enclosing transaction. If a later UPDATE fails (e.g. disk/IO error), the DB is left with all categories cleared and only some rules applied — history partially uncategorized until the user re-runs reprocess. Recoverable (idempotent re-run fixes it) and the Arc<Mutex<Connection>> single-writer (D38/D31) prevents concurrent interleave, so no torn read by other writers; the only risk is a partial failure persisting. Downgraded critical→warning: rea
fix: Take `&mut Connection`, wrap the reset + all per-rule UPDATEs in one `conn.transaction()` and commit at the end so a failure rolls back to the pre-reprocess categorization. Update the single caller signature.

title: Advanced 'match by window title' drawer saves unprefixed tokens as process rules
detail: Partly real. parseRule() returns match_field:'process' for any token without a 'title:' prefix. The Advanced drawer is labelled 'Advanced — match by window title' with placeholder 'e.g. invoice' and copy 'Prefix is optional; here it's assumed.' (lines 335,350,359-360) — implying the title prefix is the default inside this drawer. So typing 'invoice' there silently creates a PROCESS rule, contradicting the drawer's stated intent. This is a real UI/behavior mismatch. HOWEVER the second half of Codex's claim is FALSE: parseRule trims first (const t = token.trim()), and the regex /^title:\s*(.+)$/
fix: Inside this drawer, default unprefixed tokens to match_field:'title' to match the copy (add a flow flag or a second parser), OR change the copy/placeholder to state bare tokens are app-name (process) rules and only `title:` switches to title matching. The empty-pattern guard is already effectively present.

title: Toggling off a just-moved app leaves the old owner's rule in movedIds, deleting it on save
detail: Real. resolveConflict(true) does two things: adds the other category's rule id to movedIds (line 155) AND adds a pending process rule to added (line 156). If the user then clicks that pending app again, toggleApp's pending-removal branch only does setAdded(a => a.filter(...)) (line 142) — it never removes the id from movedIds. On save(), `for (const rid of movedIds) await deleteRule(rid)` (line 191) still deletes the original owner's rule, so the app ends up uncategorized even though the user backed out of the move. Bounded/recoverable (reprocess is retroactive; the user can re-add), but a gen
fix: Track the moved rule id alongside the pending add (store an optional movedRuleId on the PendingRule, or key a Map appName→movedRuleId). When removing a pending add whose move you recorded, also drop that id from movedIds.

title: Modal has max-width but no max-height/scroll — tall content can push the footer off-screen
detail: Real. The dialog div is `w-full max-w-[430px] ... bg-bg` with no max-height and no overflow on the body (line 67 `flex flex-col gap-4`). The overlay is `flex items-center justify-center p-4` with no scroll either. CategoryEditorModal embeds an app-picker grid plus chips and can grow tall; on a short window the footer row (Cancel/Save, lines 68-70) can be pushed below the viewport and become unreachable, with no scroll to recover. A real usability/responsiveness defect, but no data loss and only on short windows — warning, not critical.
fix: Add `max-h-[calc(100vh-2rem)] flex flex-col` to the dialog, keep header/footer fixed, and make the body `overflow-y-auto flex-1` so the content scrolls while the footer stays visible.

title: CANON_ORDER missing 'personal' slug — Personal sorts before Work
detail: Real. CANON_ORDER = ['deep','research','comms','breaks'] (line 33) predates D47, which added the fifth canonical slug 'personal'. The sort (lines 64-68) does CANON_ORDER.indexOf(a.slug) for slugged categories; for 'personal' indexOf returns -1, which is less than 0..3, so Personal sorts AHEAD of Work/Browsing/etc. User-created (slug null) categories correctly get 99, but a canonical slug not in the list wrongly sorts first instead of last. Cosmetic ordering bug (all categories still render) but contradicts the intended canonical order in lib/categories.ts.
fix: Derive the order from CANONICAL_CATEGORIES in lib/categories.ts (export CANON_ORDER = CANONICAL_CATEGORIES.map(c=>c.slug)) and treat indexOf === -1 as the fallback bucket (map to a large index) so unknown/canonical-not-listed slugs sort after known ones, not before.

title: Hard-coded canonical display names can disagree with user-edited DB names
detail: Real divergence. CANONICAL_CATEGORIES bakes display names (Work/Browsing/Messaging/Entertainment/Personal). D47's migration 0006 only renames FRESH installs, and CategoryEditorModal.save() calls updateCategory(category.id, trimmed, ...) for canonical categories too (line 184) — name editing is NOT blocked for built-ins (the modal only locks the colour for canonical, not the name). DayView renders the canonical Chips from the hard-coded names (lines 119-127) while the Ledger/StatTile render the backend row.name/topCategory.name (lines 105-106, 216-219). After a user renames 'Work'→'Coding', the
fix: Render legend/chip labels from backend category data (match by slug) rather than the hard-coded names; keep only slug+order in lib/categories.ts. Alternatively make canonical names non-editable in the modal so the constant stays authoritative.

title: Degraded-banner Retry refreshes day data but not the capture health check
detail: Real. The DegradedBanner's actionLabel='Retry' calls onAction={refresh} (lines 72-78), which only re-fetches day data via useDayData. captureHealthy is set solely by the effect keyed on [start] (lines 41-49), so getWatcherStatus is never re-queried by Retry. After capture recovers, the banner cannot clear until the view remounts or the date changes — Retry appears to do nothing for the very condition it's offered for. Same shape in TimelineView (lines 25-50) and WeekView (lines 30-57): captureHealthy effect keyed on [start], banner Retry wired to refresh only. Misleading UX on a real signal, n
fix: Extract a watcher-status refresh (or fold getWatcherStatus into the data refresh) and have the banner's onAction re-run both day-data refresh and the health check (setCaptureHealthy from a fresh getWatcherStatus). Apply identically in TimelineView and WeekView.

title: Browser title fallback persists fake projects (e.g. "YouTube", "GitHub") for general/non-repo pages
detail: Real bug. capture/mod.rs:214 builds ProjectSignals with cwd, url AND title together for the same event, so infer_project runs all three. classify_url returns NoSignal for GENERAL_HOSTS (youtube.com etc.) and for GITHUB_NON_REPO owners (notifications, settings…), then control falls through to classify_title unconditionally. classify_title('Video - YouTube') splits on ' - ' → candidate 'YouTube'; 'YouTube' is NOT in APP_NAMES (which holds Claude/Spotify/Slack/Finder/Notion/WhatsApp/Google Chrome/Brave), so it builds a weak Candidate canonical_key='YouTube' and resolve() persists a real project.
fix: In infer_project, suppress the title fallback whenever a URL was present (a browser event already had its best signal): track url_present and skip the `if let Some(title)` block when sig.url.is_some(). Title inference should only fire for editor/terminal events that carry no URL. Alternatively gate classify_title to known editor title shapes. Add a regression test: url=youtube.com + title='Video -

title: Distinct user-created (slugless) categories collapse together — and into Uncategorized — because category_of maps slug=None to OTHER_SLUG
detail: Real bug. db.rs confirms user-created categories have slug=None (only the canonical four deep/research/comms/breaks carry slugs — D42, get_categories test at db.rs:1465). category_of maps every slug=None category to OTHER_SLUG ('other'), the SAME slug used for category_id=None (no rule matched). Consequences keyed by slug: (1) build_day_view totals HashMap keys by slug, so two custom categories 'Design' and 'Music' (+ truly-uncategorized events) merge into one CategorySlice; the first-inserted name wins via or_insert, so the displayed name is wrong and seconds are summed across unrelated categ
fix: Give slugless categories a stable distinct identity instead of collapsing to 'other': in category_of, for a matched category with slug=None emit a synthetic per-id slug like format!("category-{id}") (carry the id into CategoryMeta or pass it through), and reserve OTHER_SLUG strictly for category_id=None (no match). The frontend then needs a colour fallback for category-<id> slugs (use the category

title: Spans crossing local midnight are counted whole on the start day and absent from the next (no range clipping)
detail: Confirmed mechanically. duration() returns end_time-start_time with no clamp; db::get_activity_logs selects WHERE start_time >= ?1 AND start_time <= ?2 (by START only); lib.rs get_day/get_week/get_timeline pass the day/week bounds and the builders never clip a span to the requested range. So a 23:50→00:10 active span (start_time before midnight) is counted entirely on the day it started and is wholly missing from the next day's read (its start_time is outside that day's window). Reachability is real but narrow: capture closes spans on every focus change, the idle gate (D39) closes long-idle sp
fix: Clip each span to the requested [start,end] before aggregation/run-building: effective_start = max(event.start_time, range_start), effective_end = min(event.end_time, range_end), secs = (effective_end-effective_start).max(0); use these clipped bounds in duration, totals, and run start/end. Either thread the range into build_day_view/build_day_slice/build_timeline, or widen the query to select span
